mnemonics: PEARSON Groups: Properties, Elements, Associativity, Identity, Symmetry, Operations, Notation
keyPoints: A group is a set equipped with a binary operation that is associative, has an identity element, and every element has an inverse.
The order of a group is the number of elements in its underlying set.
Groups can be finite or infinite, and abelian (commutative) or non-abelian.
commonMistakes: Assuming all groups are commutative (abelian); many important groups, like symmetric groups, are not.
Forgetting to check the closure property when determining if a set and operation form a group.
Confusing the identity element with the number 1 or zero, as it depends on the operation (e.g., 0 is the identity for addition, 1 for multiplication).
